name: Excoverse
short_description: Master stylish combat, takedowns and unique abilities in this fast-paced Anime Action RPG. As a noble lord, slash through enemies and make critical story decisions to save your daughter in a collapsing world. Define your legacy in this intense Hack and Slash adventure.
detailed_description:
The celestials took your daughter. To save her, you must become a monster—without betraying your people.

In a world torn apart by the discovery of the Forbidden Stone, four ancient celestial descend upon humanity, bringing ruin in their wake. Amid the chaos, a father's life is shattered when his daughter is taken—vanishing into the divine unknown.

But this is not a tale of heroes.

It’s a story of desperation, duty... and vengeance.

You are a noble lord—tasked with protecting your dying kingdom—now forced into a perilous quest through mythic lands to rescue your daughter.

Each step forward blurs the line between savior and sinner, and every celestial you confront brings you closer to the truth... or total ruin.

Stealth meets hack-and-slash combat

Eliminate enemies silently—or slice through them in high-speed, anime-style action.

Deep character progression & artifact upgrades

Unlock abilities and develop your fighting style to suit your path.

Puzzle-solving and exploration

Discover lost civilizations, ancient runes, and forbidden knowledge.

Story-driven RPG experience

Make impactful choices that affect your people, your daughter’s fate, and your own soul.

Atmospheric anime visuals & dramatic cutscenes

From shadowy forests to ruined temples, every region tells a story.

In Excoverse, your journey is more than a rescue mission—it is a reckoning.

Will you unravel the celestials true intentions and reunite your family…

Or will you become what you fear most?

supported_languages:
English*, French, Italian, German, Spanish - Spain, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese - Portugal, Simplified Chinese, Turkish, Thai
*languages with full audio support

reviews.review_text:
I'm really sorry to give it a negative review but this game is not in a 1.0 completed state. As an early access I would be fine with all of the issues but not as a full release. I won't refund and give it another try at a later point but currently I can't recommend the game at all. If you continue working on the game and start fixing some of the issues I might change it to positive but currently it needs a lot of work.

Pro:
- Performance is good
- Combat controls and animations are fun
- Graphics might not be the best but I still like them
- Mouse and Keybord controls are good

Con:
- Voice acting is really bad so far (Arella is good though) for characters that sounded like humans but the also some voices sound so hard like AI (like the entrance guard in Niafell). Also the main character voice doesn't fit his appearance at all and sounds so emotionless.
- Combat is waaaaay to easy, you can literally spam everything and don't really get hit because if you spam left mouse button you will 100 % parry.
- Level curve doesn't really exist. I was 40min in and I wasted maybe 15 mins with not doing actual gameplay because I dealt with issues and I'm level 15???
- Niafell is a lot of empty fields (reminds me of Pokemon Scarlet and Violet)
- No achievements
- Gamepad controls are not feeling that good to play with
- You get a quest and a big yellow marker shows you where to go
- Objects pop in and out a lot (even worse than in Pokemon Scarlet and Violet)



Issues and Bugs:
- If you run a stone (or a tree trunk or so) slightly up and jump at the right time you float in the air for a bit until you land
- Loading screen is super long
- Happened once so far but after restarting the game my backpack was empty at that moment
- If you save you always land back in the center island, which means if you save on one of the outer islands and you want to travel back you have to watch the animation riding the train from the island you want to go to back to the center island because the train is technically still at this island but the game respawned you in the center island.
- Had one time that after doing a ground smash attack that my screen was shaking the entire time, only fixed after using my 1st ability
- In Niafell I randomly solved one of the cube puzzles and it said 1/42 solved but now all cube puzzles are locked
- Randomly in combat my abilities got unequipped
- If you use V for drinking the potion and you have none you get a voice line which will trigger with every press so if you spam the button you trigger the line for every press (needs a cooldown)
- After reloading or restarting the game all chests are closed and you can get the loot again
- In the train ride you can spam G for the black void thing and if you move around you can glitch out of the train
- In Niafell there are some market stands with literally no people but a local sound effect with a big crowd talking
- In Niafell is an shopkeeper called Albert but his displayed name is mirrored
- Pressing Esc in the beginning cutscene opens a paused screen but doesn't pause it
- You can press Q in dialogs and it makes the effect it does outside of dialogs
- You can press G in dialogs and move around even though you can't see it
- The guard told me that Nyimura is waiting in front of the palace but there is not a single person.... so I can't continue playing right now.

I haven’t fully finished it yet, but so far the story is intriguing and keeps you curious. There are some minor bugs, but they didn’t affect my gameplay. I’ll update my review once I complete the story.

Pros:

-The graphics and atmosphere are very nice. Performance is excellent.

-Combat mechanics feel great and are easy to get into. The fluid animations and seamless combat really stood out to me.

-The decision making system is well done. Your quests change based on your choices. I haven’t tried every path yet, but I plan to replay and explore the other branches after finishing.

-Skills feel smooth and unique.

-There’s an elemental reaction system, with different combinations depending on your skills.

-The music is good.

Cons:

-There are some minor issues in interface, but they didn’t impact my gameplay.

-In the open world, you sometimes have to figure out what to do on your own. There’s often little to no guidance, so exploration is up to the player.

-Unlocking skills takes a bit too long. After 2 hours of looting, I was only able to unlock 2 skills. It could be a bit easier.

-The parry system is currently quite easy it could be more challenging.

Went in without expecting much, but this actually surprised me in a good way.

The combat is easily the highlight. It’s fast, responsive, and just feels good to play. Animations flow nicely into each other, and chaining abilities feels smooth instead of clunky.

The world has a nice vibe too. The open world can feel a bit empty at times and you’re mostly left to explore things on your own, but I personally enjoy that kind of discovery driven gameplay, so it worked for me.

I think the game could benefit from a higher difficulty option though. Right now it feels a bit too easy, and adding more challenge would make the combat even more satisfying.

You can really feel the passion behind this game. It’s always great to see indie developers creating unique experiences like this.
It may not be perfect, but it has its own charm and is definitely worth supporting. If you enjoy seamless combat, this is definitely for you.
